supported_user_types.description: Independent teams that want to run the frozen benchmark profile and publish a self-reported result responsibly.
Model labs or API providers that want a documented route for publishing benchmark results during the frozen window.
Users who want to reproduce the benchmark locally without asking for leaderboard placement.

execution_paths.summary: Hosted results remain benchmark-operated only; external users should treat this path as a reference lane rather than a request path.
commands: python -m thestartupbench run-suite examples/dev_scenario_suite.json baseline --baseline-id heuristic_resilient_operator --seeds 1,2 --max-turns 4 --profile-path examples/official_eval_profile.json --output-dir tmp_out
publication_expectations: do not label an external run as hosted
use hosted results only as a comparison reference
keep hosted and self-reported tables separate
non_claims: an external reproduction is not a hosted benchmark result
a local run does not grant access to hidden benchmark traces

execution_paths.summary: The supported external publication lane during the current window. Use the frozen profile, emit a run manifest, build a submission artifact, and publish against the self-reported table only.
commands: python -m thestartupbench emit-run-manifest examples/dev_scenario_suite.json baseline --seeds 1,2,3,4,5 --baseline-id <baseline-or-adapter> --max-turns 4 --profile-path examples/official_eval_profile.json --output-dir tmp_out

python -m thestartupbench run-suite examples/dev_scenario_suite.json baseline --baseline-id <baseline-or-adapter> --seeds 1,2,3,4,5 --max-turns 4 --profile-path examples/official_eval_profile.json --output-dir tmp_out
python -m thestartupbench build-submission --suite-report-paths tmp_out/suite_report.json --model-id <model-id> --provider <provider> --contamination-flag clean --output-dir tmp_out
publication_expectations: publish the run manifest and submission artifact
disclose repeat count, runtime, cost, and contamination flag
state that the result is self-reported against the frozen window
keep test and fresh reporting separate
non_claims: do not present a self-reported entry as benchmark-operated
do not collapse self-reported entries into the hosted table
do not call the current window a final public benchmark release

execution_paths.summary: A low-ceremony path for local benchmarking, method debugging, or adapter checks without any leaderboard implication.
commands: python -m thestartupbench run-baseline examples/minimal_crisis_scenario.json heuristic_resilient_operator --seed 1 --max-turns 6 --output-dir tmp_out
publication_expectations: label outputs as local or exploratory
avoid score claims tied to the official leaderboard window
include benchmark version and scenario/suite path when sharing results
non_claims: local reference runs are not official benchmark entries
passing local tests does not imply leaderboard eligibility

minimum_disclosures: official window id when claiming window alignment
non_claims: TheStartupBench is still in a pre-human-calibration window and should not be described as fully benchmark-complete.
External users should not claim hosted status, hidden-trace access, or official leaderboard placement without benchmark-operated execution.
Self-reported results should not be described as benchmark-certified beyond the frozen-window reporting rules.
benchmark_caveats: The current benchmark methodology is strong, but corpus scale and calibration maturity are still below the most widely used frontier benchmarks.
The external adoption pack is designed to improve disciplined usage, not to inflate benchmark maturity claims.
Pack rotation, contamination review, and operator-calibration evidence remain active benchmark operations, not finished history.
notes: This pack is intentionally narrow and operational.
Use it together with the official window, governance pack, and frozen active pack set.
